summary: Parallel review: FAIL (1 critical, 8 warning, 9 suggestion). Compound gate: PASS

findingsList.text: 🟡 `bin/lib/runbooks.mjs:110` — `isSafeRegex` heuristic only catches `(a+)+`-style ReDoS; more exotic patterns slip through. Consider a timeout wrapper as fallback.
🟡 `bin/lib/runbooks.mjs:247` — `matchRunbook` tie-break is a dense one-liner. Extract a named comparator for readability.
🟡 `bin/lib/run.mjs:460` — Spec-parsed tasks lack `hint: null`, unlike runbook tasks. Asymmetric shape.
🟡 `test/runbooks.test.mjs:978` — Contract test counts pre-resolution `rb.tasks.length`, which includes `include` entries as tasks.

🟡 test/runbooks.test.mjs:984 — Contract test counts raw `rb.tasks.length` not resolved count; a runbook with 3 tasks + 1 `include` pointing to an empty target would pass >=4 despite only 3 resolved tasks. Add a resolved-count assertion or document as intentional.

🟡 `bin/lib/runbooks.mjs:110` — Pre-existing: `isSafeRegex` misses alternation-based ReDoS like `(a|a)+$` (confirmed 3.7s with 25 chars); consider `safe-regex2` or `re2` (carried from task-10-sec, not new to this task)
🟡 test/runbooks.test.mjs:399 — Duplicate test: "warns on nested includes" is identical logic to "does not recurse into nested includes" (line 338). Neither captures `console.warn`. Backlog item.
